title: Shinji and Asuka in comatose
wikitext:
[]: Shinji and Asuka in comatose.             Quote Post by Kye7 » Tue Sep 20, 2011 5:05 pm This is for mature audiences, obviously. I don't quite understand this. We all know at the beginning of EoE, Shinji masturbates over Auskas body (Breasts) and says "I'm so fucked up" Does he do this because he likes her? Or because he can't show his 'love' in any other way? I remember somewhere in the part of the series Asuka is yelling at Shinji for 'not really liking her, and just wanting her for his own purposes' (Own purposes obviously being sex) Can someone elaborate on this? And here's the part where it really gets me, I don't know if this is in his own mind, or if she's actually saying this to him: I know all about your little jerk-off fantasies about me. Go ahead, and do it like you always do... I'll even stand here and watch you. But... if I can't have you all to myself, then I don't want ANYTHING from you.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Sep 20, 2011 5:08 pm 1. Shinji does this to justify seeing himself as horrid. 2. This is in his mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Kye7 » Tue Sep 20, 2011 5:16 pm Stryker wrote: 1. Shinji does this to justify seeing himself as horrid. 2. This is in his mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 20, 2011 5:35 pm The objective-world coffee-pot sequence is an addition at the start of the director's cut of episode 24 where Asuka learns of Kaji's death - and then she is next seen in that bathtub. Asuka knows about Shinji's onanistic habits by the same mechanism as they both learn about Misato and Kaji's week in bed -- all minds become one mind, as Gendo explains in episode 25.

[]:             Quote Post by Kye7 » Tue Sep 20, 2011 6:07 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The objective-world coffee-pot sequence is an addition at the start of the director's cut of episode 24 where Asuka learns of Kaji's death - and then she is next seen in that bathtub. Asuka knows about Shinji's onanistic habits by the same mechanism as they both learn about Misato and Kaji's week in bed -- all minds become one mind , as Gendo explains in episode 25.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 20, 2011 6:58 pm Kye7 wrote: When you say this, is this after instrumentality? So everybody (and anybody) has access to everybody's and anybody's thoughts/memories?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Sep 20, 2011 7:19 pm This is actually a Pre-Instrumentality, apparently arranged by Rei/Lilith, that only includes Shinji, Asuka, Misato, and Ritsuko. After returning from this, Shinji says: "Everyone Die!", triggering Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Sep 20, 2011 7:50 pm Yes, Asuka's arm was split in half. By the way, I prefer dubs also, but watch EoE again with subtitles. There are a lot of plotholes in the dub caused by mistranslations.

[]:             Quote Post by ipwnuall » Tue Sep 20, 2011 8:20 pm I had always assumed that she found out when they all went tang.

[]:             Quote Post by Kye7 » Tue Sep 20, 2011 9:01 pm thewayneiac wrote: This is actually a Pre-Instrumentality, apparently arranged by Rei/Lilith, that only includes Shinji, Asuka, Misato, and Ritsuko. After returning from this, Shinji says: "Everyone Die!", triggering Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Sep 20, 2011 9:08 pm Kye7 wrote: How do Shinji's own words trigger third impact? In my point of view, that's like saying "I want school to be over!" but obviously it isn't going to happen, because that action is in the hands of a greater power (EG: principal/School board/whatever) So how exactly do those words, trigger third impact? I thought that was in control of Rei. (I will never understand Rei, such a beautiful yet mysterious character) It was cut in half because her sync ratio was so high, correct? This also explains why massive amounts of blood are coming from her eye-socket. But as her Eva (Unit-02) is disembowled... Why isn't Asuka herself disemboweled? ------------ I will most likely watch it again this weekend, paying even more attention to minute details and with subtitles Also.. Is the final line "You disgust me" or "I feel sick" ?

[]:             Quote Post by merri » Tue Sep 20, 2011 9:18 pm Trajan wrote: 1. Rei has control over Instrumentality but decides to leave it up to Shinji as to what should happen. It's like the principal asking you if you want school to be canceled or not. Shinji decides that everyone should die...so everyone dies. Sort of. 2. The plug suit would conceivably cover up the wounds. Plus it's red, so blood stains would be harder to see and she's clearly writhing in pain. 3. It's "I feel sick."

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Tue Sep 20, 2011 9:49 pm merri wrote: Problem with that response to her disembowelment is that the Eva's face is also cannibalized and the eyes have been removed from their sockets, when Asuka-while writing in pain-clearly still has skin, eyes, and the like.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 20, 2011 11:04 pm Kye7 wrote: It was cut in half because her sync ratio was so high, correct?

[]:             Quote Post by merri » Wed Sep 21, 2011 12:46 am Azathoth wrote: Which is what you'd expect, but Eva is laughably inconsistent about sync ratio and how it affects the pilot in general. Some people have made a case that the Lance(s) of Longinus intensify the effects of sync ratio, which would explain why Asuka's arm and eye are damaged but not her stomach or...rest of her. "kimochi warui", which could just as well be translated as either of the above. Literally it just means "feels bad", it's a common expression in Japanese for any negative feeling. My favored translation is "I feel terrible", but really there's no perfect rendering of it and all we have to go on is that one ambiguous phrase and Anno's direction in the script that the line should be read "in the coldest voice possible".

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:22 am Azathoth wrote: One would think that, even in her mental state, she would still voice some objection to Shinji jacking it right next to her if she were conscious.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:41 am CJD wrote: This is Asuka , she probably thinks it's a compliment, from Shinji no less.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:58 am CJD wrote: don't we get an image of Asuka's corpse rotting? Is that from EoE or Episode 22?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Sep 21, 2011 2:03 am ^ Bah, no luck then, sorry.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Sep 21, 2011 11:21 am As noted in other threads, Asuka wasn't comatose -- she was sedated. The difference is moot for the purposes of this topic but it's a common error so that's why I mention it. Anyway, regarding Asuka's eye and arm: this wasn't the result of high sync. We've seen Evas lose limbs with no ill effects for the pilot, and we would expect additional wounds on Asuka if sync alone was responsible for her injuries. It's worth remembering that the Lance of Longinus was designed with but a single purpose: to control/disable Seeds of Life. This makes it a fantastic weapon for use against Evas and Angels alike, since it cuts through AT fields like they aren't even there. The replicas of the LoL used by the Mass Production Evangelions appear to share this quality. Taken in isolation it's not very helpful, but when you combine it with synchronization everything falls into place. When an Eva and its pilot are synced their AT fields are also synced. A normal wound wouldn't have any effect on the pilot's AT field, but a wound from the Lance would cut through the Eva's AT field and, thanks to synchronization, damage the pilot's field in a similar fashion (which is exactly what we see in Asuka's case). She isn't disembowelled because the MPEs' teeth don't share the Lance's properties; only wounds by the replica Lances would injure her. And in reference to Az's comment I think it's more a case of sync allowing the Lances to affect Asuka's AT field than of the Lances intensifying the effects of sync for whatever reason. Whether the sync rate was 20 or 400 the effects would be the same IMO. If we assume the bandages Asuka wears post-3I are covering wounds of some sort it's likely she has additional wounds/scars/whatever across her torso and legs from the additional Lance hits at the end of her battle. We souldn't see these because of her plugsuit. This also explains why she has these wounds after 3I, even though she probably should have come out of the LCL fully healed: her AT field is still damaged. Reconstituting onself from LCL would allow you to heal all physical ailments, but a damaged AT field is another matter. Will it eventually recover? Dunno. We've never seen anything survive a Lance hit in the past, so there's no telling what will happen going forward. All is thus explained.

Article: Shinji and Asuka in comatose
's why I mention it. Anyway, regarding Asuka's eye and arm: this wasn't the result of high sync. We've seen Evas lose limbs with no ill effects for the pilot, and we would expect additional wounds on Asuka if sync alone was responsible for her injuries. It's worth remembering that the Lance of Longinus was designed with but a single purpose: to control/disable Seeds of Life. This makes it a fantastic weapon for use against Evas and Angels alike, since it cuts through AT fields like they aren't even there. The replicas of the LoL used by the Mass Production Evangelions appear to share this quality. Taken in isolation it's not very helpful, but when you combine it with synchronization everything falls into place. When an Eva and its pilot are synced their AT fields are also synced. A normal wound wouldn't have any effect on the pilot's AT field, but a wound from the Lance would cut through the Eva's AT field and, thanks to synchronization, damage the pilot's field in a similar fashion (which is exactly what we see in Asuka's case). She isn't disembowelled because the MPEs' teeth don't share the Lance's properties; only wounds by the replica Lances would injure her. And in reference to Az's comment I think it's more a case of sync allowing the Lances to affect Asuka's AT field than of the Lances intensifying the effects of sync for whatever reason. Whether the sync rate was 20 or 400 the effects would be the same IMO. If we assume the bandages Asuka wears post-3I are covering wounds of some sort it's likely she has additional wounds/scars/whatever across her torso and legs from the additional Lance hits at the end of her battle. We souldn't see these because of her plugsuit. This also explains why she has these wounds after 3I, even though she probably should have come out of the LCL fully healed: her AT field is still damaged. Reconstituting onself from LCL would allow you to heal all physical ailments, but a damaged AT field is another matter. Will it eventually recover? Dunno. We've never seen anything survive a Lance hit in the past, so there's no telling what will happen going forward. All is thus explained.
